_comment: High-frequency closed-class function words: conjunctions (CONJ), postpositions (ADP),
particles (PART), and adverbs (ADV). Most are INDECLINABLE — they do not inflect — so
each is enumerated as a whole `irregular` surface (lemma == surface, empty morphemes),
consulted by the analyzer BEFORE the FSM. They are dictionary-verified: source=lexicon,
never a guess.
Why `irregular`, not regular `entries` (for the indeclinables):
- The analyzer's _generate() only walks the nominal/verbal graphs (NOMINAL_POS/VERBAL_POS);
CONJ/ADP/PART are neither, so a plain regular entry would be dead data and the word would
still fall to the guesser.
- Routing them through the nominal FSM (e.g. by widening NOMINAL_POS) would OVERGENERATE:
*amalar, *gibiye, *fakattan would become lexicon-verified, and run-on misspellings like
'belkide'/'yinede' would silently become confident locative parses. The frozen-irregular
design keeps every non-standard form an honest guess. The negative tests lock this in.
DECLINING function words live in the regular `entries` list (below), not `irregular`, and
carry an attribute that routes them — in the analyzer's start-state selection, NOT a hard-
coded surface list — to a dedicated FSM start state whose only edges are the ek-fiil copula/
persons. There are three:
- the INTERROGATIVE PARTICLE mi/mı/mu/mü (attribute 'interrogative' -> Q_ROOT): mi/midir/
misin/miyim/miydi/miymiş parse (lemma 'mi', question=True). Each of the four harmonic
surfaces is its own entry (via `forms`) so harmony follows that surface's own vowel
(mu+sun -> musun); all four share lemma 'mi'.
- the NEGATIVE COPULA değil (attribute 'negative_copula' -> NEG_COP_ROOT): değildi/değildim/
değilim/değilsin/değildir/değilse/değilmiş/değiller parse (lemma 'değil', PART, POLARITY=
negative — inherent, never overwritten). It is the negative mirror of the ek-fiil güzeldim.
- the SUBSTANTIVE VERB i- (attribute 'substantive_verb' -> I_ROOT): the ek-fiil written as a
separate word (idi/idim/imiş/ise/isem/iken), lemma 'i', pos AUX. Bufferless (idi, not
*iydi); I_ROOT is non-final (no bare 'i'). 'geliyor idi' / 'güzel ise' now analyze.
Each dedicated state takes NO plural/possessive/case (these are particles/copulas, not nouns:
no *miler, no *değile, no *ide). None may ALSO be listed as `irregular`: irregulars are
prepended un-deduped, which would double the bare surface and hide its feature behind an
empty-feature row (so değil is NOT in the `irregular` list below — moved here this milestone).
The homograph 'ise' keeps its frozen topic-particle irregular row (lemma 'ise', 'as for',
primary because irregulars are prepended); the i+se conditional reading coexists as an alt.
Homographs are PRESERVED, never erased. The analyzer prepends an irregular above the regular
parses without deleting them, so:
- 'de'  -> PART (primary), but the verb de- imperative reading survives as an alternative
(the verb 'de' stays in verbs.json; do NOT remove it).
- 'göre' -> ADP (primary), gör-+optative survives; 'bile' -> PART, bil-+optative survives;
- 'birden' -> ADV (primary), bir(NUM)+ablative survives;
- 'müdür' -> NOUN (director) stays primary, mü+DIr (assertive, lemma 'mi') survives as an alt.
Making the particle/postposition the primary for these bare surfaces is a context-free
frequency choice (the function-word reading is far more common in running text).
Harmony variants are SEPARATE orthographic words and are each listed, all sharing one lemma:
- the question particle mi / mı / mu / mü  -> lemma 'mi'   (regular `entries`, declining)
- the clitic de / da                       -> lemma 'de'   (`irregular`, frozen)
Circumflex is not folded by normalize() (â != a), so the 'still' spelling 'hâlâ' is listed
separately with lemma 'hala'.
DEFERRED (documented, fall to the guesser — correct per contract, defer over a wrong rule):
- değilken, idiyse, and -DIrlAr stacking (değildirler); clitic-vs-word unification for i-;
- karşıya/karşıda etc. (would need a karşı NOUN entry); inflected tekrar/artık noun readings.
(The copular inflection of değil — değildi/değilim — and the substantive verb i- are now
IMPLEMENTED via the declining `entries` above; they are no longer deferred.)
Note the asymmetry with the pre-existing ADV entries in other.json (çok/az/şimdi/bugün) which
DO inflect via NOMINAL_POS: the new ADVs are deliberately frozen so run-on misspellings stay
guesses instead of becoming false lexicon-verified locatives (belkide, yinede).
